intro: BoxyHQ is paid subscription; Corbado is priced on request. BoxyHQ: Identity / Auth / User Management tool for developers. Specializes in Enterprise SSO. Corbado: Identity / Auth / User Management tool for developers. Specializes in Passkey Authentication. BoxyHQ fits apps, B2B — in practice, apps needing SSO/MFA. Corbado fits apps, B2B — in practice, apps needing SSO/MFA. On our rubric BoxyHQ scores 5/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency, while Corbado scores 3/5 and 2/5. The honest trade-off: BoxyHQ's main drawback — Internal tools with basic auth; Corbado's — Internal tools with basic auth. BoxyHQ is explicitly not the right pick for internal, Static projects. Corbado is not aimed at internal, Static projects. Another BoxyHQ advantage: B2B requiring SAML/SCIM. Another BoxyHQ friction point: Static sites without user accounts. Our read on BoxyHQ: best for apps needing sso/mfa. BoxyHQ offers subscription from $770. On Corbado: best for apps needing sso/mfa. Corbado offers $1. BoxyHQ runs on paid subscription pricing with low lock-in, Corbado on priced on request with medium lock-in.
quick_take: BoxyHQ is for apps, B2B; Corbado is for apps, B2B; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose BoxyHQ if your work looks like apps needing SSO/MFA, and if portability and keeping the exit door open matter.
verdict_b: Choose Corbado if your work looks like apps needing SSO/MFA, and if you're comfortable requesting a quote before committing.

faq.a: Corbado's pricing isn't published in our data, so we can't give a definitive comparison. BoxyHQ uses paid subscription pricing. You'll need to request a Corbado quote to compare directly.

faq.q: Can I migrate from BoxyHQ to Corbado?
faq.a: Migrating out of BoxyHQ is straightforward — it's rated low lock-in (OSS enterprise SSO). The harder question is how deeply you commit to Corbado afterward, since it sits at medium lock-in (Passkey auth, standards-based). Plan the forward cost, not just the exit.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, BoxyHQ or Corbado?
faq.a: BoxyHQ scores higher in our rubric (5/5 vs 3/5 for Corbado). Transparency is 4/5 for BoxyHQ and 2/5 for Corbado. DX scores are rubric-based, not benchmarks, so evaluate against your own toolchain.

faq.q: Is Corbado a good alternative to BoxyHQ?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — Corbado is a plausible alternative for many BoxyHQ use cases. It fits best when apps, B2B. Skip it if internal, Static projects.
